claims.text: Three difficulty tiers (intro / intermediate / advanced), 5,000 leads each.
Each tier has 1,500 accounts and 4,200 contacts.
Public bundles ship 7 snapshot-safe relational tables (accounts, contacts, leads, touches, sessions, sales_activities, opportunities).
Instructor companion ships 9 tables (the 7 public ones plus customers and subscriptions).
Conversion rate (cross-seed median, seeds 42-46): intro 42.67%, intermediate 22.27%, advanced 7.87%.
Cross-seed median LR AUC: intro 0.671, intermediate 0.670, advanced 0.563.
Cross-seed median LR Average Precision: intro 0.568, intermediate 0.358, advanced 0.093.
Cross-seed median P@100: intro 0.62, intermediate 0.41, advanced 0.07.
Cross-seed median Brier score: intro 0.222, intermediate 0.163, advanced 0.073.
Conversion-rate, AP, and P@100 orderings hold intro > intermediate > advanced.
Difficulty knobs by tier: signal strength 0.90/0.70/0.50, noise scale 0.10/0.30/0.55, missing rate 2%/8%/18%.
GBM-LR AUC delta is negative in every tier (-0.022 / -0.018 / -0.030); v1's snapshot is dominated by linear features.
lead_source is weakly informative — out-of-sample univariate AUC ~0.50-0.52 across tiers, per-channel rate spread <=0.05.
Cohort-shift AUC degradation is small (v1 has no time-of-year drift baked in).
Public leads.parquet drops conversion_timestamp and converted_within_90_days.
Public opportunities.parquet drops close_outcome and closed_at.
Public bundles omit customers and subscriptions tables entirely.
Snapshot-filtered event tables (touches, sessions, sales_activities, opportunities) keep only rows with <ts> <= lead_created_at + snapshot_day.
total_touches_all is the deliberate leakage trap: it counts touches over the full 90-day window and is flagged leakage_risk=True.
Splits are 70/15/15 train/valid/test, deterministic given seed; recorded in tasks/converted_within_90_days/task_manifest.json.
Splitter keyed on lead_id only — 518/557 (~93%) of test accounts also appear in train on the intermediate bundle. Use GroupKFold(account_id) for a generalisation-faithful number.
Recipe b2b_saas_procurement_v1, canonical seed 42, cross-seed sweep 42-46, bundle schema version 5, package leadforge 1.0.0+.
Every file in the bundle is SHA-256 hashed in manifest.json; the bundle is verifiable end-to-end with `leadforge validate`.
Acceptance bands for every gate live as YAML at release/docs/v1_acceptance_gates_bands.yaml; bands are recipe gates, not achievable ranges.
Intended uses: teaching baseline lead scoring, relational feature engineering, leakage detection, calibration / lift / P@K / value-aware ranking, model-family comparison under a controlled DGP.
Out of scope: production lead scoring, vendor benchmarking, causal-inference research requiring DGP recovery, demographic / fairness research.

notes: This register is rendered from release/claims_register_source.yaml. Every claim in release/README.md should appear here.  Agents and CI can use the (backing_artifact, backing_path) tuple to locate the source-of-truth value without parsing prose.

schema.backing_artifact: Path within the published bundle (or repo) that carries the source of truth.  ``<tier>`` is a placeholder for intro / intermediate / advanced.
schema.backing_path: JSON-path / YAML-path / column reference inside the backing artifact, or ``n/a`` for prose contracts and whole-file claims.
schema.category: One of: composition, calibration, redaction, difficulty, limitations, splits, provenance, out_of_scope, intended_use.
schema.id: Short stable identifier; quoted in CI failure messages.
schema.text: The claim as it appears in the README (verbatim, where practical).
schema.verifier: Free-form name of the script / probe / test that re-derives the claim end-to-end.  ``n/a`` means the claim is a prose contract that is not mechanically verifiable.
